problem_statement: Expected: the library bookmobile planner schedules all 312 eligible stops from the approved address export. Observed: only 19 stops are scheduled, with most omitted addresses located in postal zones whose five-digit codes begin with zero. Diagnose the causal family and evidence sufficiency without changing the planner or any operational records.

observations.statement: The immutable approved export contains 312 rows and defines postal_code as five-character text; sampled omitted records contain values such as 02108 and 02109.

A read-only profile of the planner's captured batch shows 293 postal codes with four digits, including 2108 and 2109, and no value retaining an initial zero; row count and address text otherwise match the approved export.

An isolated run holds planner revision, settings, clock, route map, and dependency stubs fixed: the archived text-preserving export schedules 312 stops, while the captured batch fingerprint schedules 19.
A geofence filter release occurred two days earlier, but a read-only diff shows no postal parsing change and the same revision schedules all stops when given the archived export.
Retrying the captured batch in a fresh isolated process produces the same 19-stop plan, despite the original worker log containing a transient memory-pressure warning.
